creator: royger@freebsd.org
text:
(In reply to Zhenlei Huang from comment #25)
Thanks for the analysis.

I think there either needs to be a way to signal that the package checksum should be ignored (and thus assume the checksum to be correct), or there must be a point in the software stack where a checksum is calculated for packets that don't have one if further processing requires such checksum.

Thanks, Roger.
